marketState: neutral-to-bullish, weather- and positioning-driven short-squeeze setup capped by storage surplus
summary: Front-month NG ($2.928) sits almost exactly on modeled fair value at every horizon (all spreads within a few cents, status 'FAIR'), so there is no standalone valuation edge. The dominant signal is weather: the 8-16 day demand outlook is +45.7% vs its 5-year average and above the entire historical range (rangePos 1.34), pointing to a significant heat-driven cooling-demand surge, while 1-7 day demand is only mildly elevated. Managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of the past 5 years, an unusually stretched position that, combined with incoming heat, creates real short-covering squeeze risk. Working against upside, storage is +183 Bcf above the 5-year average with this week's build beating forecast (bearish surprise), and daily/weekly technicals remain in a confirmed downtrend (weekly RSI 37.7, bearish EMA cross on nearly every timeframe). Seasonality adds a mild bullish tilt (69% probability of higher prices in 4 weeks, +3.7% typical move) but its reliability is explicitly flagged low this week, so it's treated as a tiebreaker rather than a primary driver.

longTerm.entry: $2.85-2.95 (scale in on dips toward fair-value midpoint)
longTerm.rationale: Extreme 8-16D heat/demand (+45.7% vs 5y, above the historical max) into peak cooling season, plus managed-money net shorts at a 5y-percentile of just 8, argue for mean-reversion/squeeze upside over a multi-week window; the fair-value band ($2.42-$3.51 across horizons) has plenty of room above current price. Storage's +183 Bcf surplus and a bearish build surprise cap how far this can run and keep conviction at medium rather than high.
longTerm.invalidation: Storage surprises keep coming in bearish/large while the 8-16D heat forecast fades in updated model runs, or price closes below $2.65 confirming the weekly downtrend overrides the squeeze setup.

shortTerm.sizing: 0.5R approx 0.5% account risk (low conviction, against the prevailing daily/weekly trend)
shortTerm.rationale: Price is fairly valued now and at +7D (no cheap/expensive edge), and daily/weekly technicals are bearish (RSI 41/38, EMA death cross on most timeframes), arguing for caution. But the tail end of this window overlaps the extreme 8-16D heat surge, and record-low COT positioning for managed money raises the odds of a fast short-covering pop, so a small, tightly-stopped long is favored over a trend-following short.
shortTerm.invalidation: A close back below $2.78 (recent range low) with the heat forecast fading would confirm the downtrend and invalidate the squeeze thesis.

keyDrivers: 8-16D demand forecast +45.7% vs 5y, above the entire historical range - major heat-driven bullish catalyst
Managed-money net short at 5y-percentile 8 - stretched positioning, squeeze risk
Storage +183 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish build surprise (+17 Bcf) - caps rallies
Daily/weekly technicals in a confirmed downtrend (weekly RSI 37.7, bearish EMA crosses)
Price sits at fair value across all horizons - no independent cheap/expensive signal
risks: Seasonality reliability flagged low this week (63pp 10-90% spread) - the seasonal pattern may not hold
8-16D heat forecast could soften in later model runs, removing the main bullish driver
Persistent storage surpluses could reinforce the bearish technical trend and trap long positions
